status: exact cofinal-degree polar closure with open terminal theorem
proof_boundary: This artifact proves that cofinally many hyperbolic Jensen degrees at each fixed shift suffice for all finite degrees. It does not prove any unbounded terminal-degree sequence for the zeta coefficients, an all-shift cofinal theorem, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

exact.cofinal_closure: If {D:H_(D,n)} is unbounded for a fixed n, then H_(d,n) holds for every finite d.
exact.all_shift_cofinal_closure: If the cofinal condition holds for every n>=0, then every shifted Jensen polynomial is hyperbolic.

rows.claim: At each fixed shift, all Jensen degrees use one common normalized coefficient sequence.
Dropping one Jensen degree is exactly the polar derivative with pole zero.
For a negative-root hyperbolic terminal polynomial, the polar quotient is an average of simple reciprocal factors.
The zero-pole polar derivative preserves negative-root hyperbolicity and interlaces the terminal roots.
Nonroot polar zeros are simple, while a nonzero terminal root loses exactly one unit of multiplicity.
One hyperbolic terminal degree closes every lower degree at the same shift by repeated polar descent.
rows.proof_boundary: Finite degree tower at one shift only.

rows.claim: An unbounded set of hyperbolic terminal degrees is equivalent to all finite degrees at a fixed shift.
rows.formula: If {D:H_(D,n)} is unbounded for a fixed n, then H_(d,n) holds for every finite d.

rows.claim: Cofinal terminal hyperbolicity at every shift suffices for the complete Jensen-window family.
rows.formula: If the cofinal condition holds for every n>=0, then every shifted Jensen polynomial is hyperbolic.
rows.proof_boundary: Conditional all-shift reduction; no terminal theorem is supplied.

rows.claim: Current rigorous finite hyperbolicity data reach every degree from 3 through 12; the separate contraction cache is not needed to infer those finite root counts.
rows.formula: 1050 Sturm rows at d=3..12; 2875 separate contraction-only rows at d=3..12
rows.proof_boundary: Finite evidence classification only; not a cofinal terminal sequence.

rows.claim: Prove hyperbolicity for an unbounded sequence of terminal degrees at every shift, with enough uniformity to survive the heat-flow argument.
rows.formula: for every n, construct D_j->infinity with H_(D_j,n)
rows.proof_boundary: Open cofinal terminal theorem; not PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
